raw_response:
I **agree**, as I see no compelling reasons to refute the assertion made regarding T-lymphocyte involvement in periodontal disease. Additionally, it aligns well with current immunological models explaining host-pathogen interactions leading to chronic inflammation typical of periodontitis. Therefore,

My final answer remains **Option C**: T-tymphocytes sensitized to bacterial plaque antigens. This option best supports the notion of active cellular immunity playing a crucial role in pathogenesis via specific recognition mechanisms.
